title: Dos and Don'ts block: optional cover (image or component-embed)
description: Add the optional cover control to the `dos-donts` NodeView (built in TASK-72): add/remove a cover, switch its kind between `image` and `component-embed`, and edit it. The cover is optional per the project owner's decision — a block with no cover is valid and common.
acceptanceCriteria: An "add cover" control appears when `cover` is `null`; once added, a way to remove it entirely (round-trips back to `cover: null`)
A kind switch toggles between `image` and `component-embed` covers

Image cover: `src` and `alt` text inputs (URL-based — this project has no generic image upload pipeline; the only upload path is the CI-authenticated preview artifact upload from TASK-43, which is not reused here)

Component-embed cover: reuses `ComponentEmbedPicker` (same picker used by the standalone `component-embed` block) to select `componentName`/`variantId`, and renders the same loading/empty/live iframe states as the standalone `ComponentEmbed` NodeView (TASK-47/51)
Cover attrs persist via `updateAttributes` and round-trip through `serialize.ts`'s `cover` shape (TASK-71) on save/reload

steps.details: In the `DosDonts` NodeView, add a toggle: no cover → "Adicionar cover" button; cover present → a way to remove it (sets `cover: null` via `updateAttributes`).
A small switcher between `image`/`component-embed`. Image kind renders two inputs (`src`, `alt`) bound to `updateAttributes({ cover: { kind: 'image', src, alt } })`.

Embed `ComponentEmbedPicker` for selection. For rendering, reuse the loading/empty/live state logic from `apps/admin/src/features/editor/nodes/ComponentEmbed.tsx` (`componentPreviews.getLatest` lookup + `data-preview-state`) — extract a small shared helper if that avoids duplicating the state machine, or duplicate minimally with a comment pointing at the canonical version if extraction isn't clean; judgment call for whoever implements, but don't silently diverge the two state machines.

Add an image cover (src/alt), publish, reload, confirm it persists. Add a component-embed cover selecting a real published preview (reuse a fixture from the connector's published previews, as in TASK-47/48's checks), confirm the iframe renders. Remove the cover, confirm it's gone after reload.

technicalNotes: Explicit scope decision: image cover is URL-only, no file upload. Building a generic image upload/storage pipeline is out of scope and materially larger than this task — flag it as a separate future task if the project owner wants it later.
`ComponentEmbedPicker` already supports an `initial` selection for re-selecting over an existing embed (TASK-48) — reuse that same prop when editing an existing component-embed cover.
